Module com.example.escriturarapida
Package com.example.escriturarapida.utilities

Class AudioManager

java.lang.Object
com.example.escriturarapida.utilities.AudioManager
public class AudioManager extends Object
Manages background music playback for the application by dividing a single audio file into named sections and switching between them based on the current game state.

The audio file is expected to contain all music tracks concatenated in one resource. Each game state maps to a specific time window [start, end] within that file, which is looped indefinitely until the state changes.

State transitions are driven externally: callers update the public static field currentStatus and then invoke update() to apply the change. update() is idempotent â€” if the state has not changed since the last call, playback is left untouched.

Typical usage:


 String url = getClass().getResource("/audio/soundtrack.mp3").toExternalForm();
 AudioManager audio = new AudioManager(url);

 // Later, when entering the game:
 AudioManager.currentStatus = AudioManager.GAME_START;
 audio.update();
Since:
2026
Version:
1.0

  • Field Details

    • mediaPlayer

      private final javafx.scene.media.MediaPlayer mediaPlayer
      The underlying JavaFX media player used for playback. A single instance is reused across all state transitions.

    • MENU

      public static final int MENU
      Audio state constant for the main menu screen.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

      public static final int GAME_START
      Audio state constant for the early stage of a game session.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • GAME_START_2

      public static final int GAME_START_2
      Audio state constant for the second phase of the game start.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • GAME_MIDDLE

      public static final int GAME_MIDDLE
      Audio state constant for the middle stage of a game session.

      • Constant Field Values

    • GAME_FINAL

      public static final int GAME_FINAL
      Audio state constant for the final stage of a game session.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • currentStatus

      public static int currentStatus
      The currently desired audio state. Must be set to one of the state constants (MENU, GAME_START, etc.) before calling update().

      Note: this field is static and therefore shared across all instances. Applications should maintain only one AudioManager at a time to avoid conflicts.

    • previousState

      private int previousState
      Tracks the state that was active during the last update() call. Used to detect changes and avoid redundant seeks or restarts. Initialized to -1 so the first update() always plays.

    • musicMenu

      private static final double[] musicMenu
      Time window for the menu music section, in seconds.

    • musicGameStart

      private static final double[] musicGameStart
      Time window for the game-start music section, in seconds.

    • musicGameStart2

      private static final double[] musicGameStart2
      Time window for the second game-start music section, in seconds.

    • musicGameMiddle

      private static final double[] musicGameMiddle
      Time window for the mid-game music section, in seconds.

    • musicGameFinal

      private static final double[] musicGameFinal
      Time window for the final-game music section, in seconds.

  • Constructor Details

    • AudioManager

      public AudioManager(String audioRoute)
      Creates a new AudioManager backed by the audio file at the given URL.
      Parameters:
      audioRoute - the URI string of the audio resource (e.g. obtained via getClass().getResource(...).toExternalForm()); must be a valid JavaFX Media URI

  • Method Details

    • playSection

      private void playSection(double[] section)
      Stops the current playback, seeks to the start of the given time window, and begins looping that section indefinitely.
      Parameters:
      section - a two-element array where section[0] is the start time and section[1] is the stop time, both in seconds

    • update

      public void update()
      Applies the current audio state if it has changed since the last call.

      Compares currentStatus against the internally tracked previousState. If they differ, the corresponding music section is played via playSection(double[]). If the state is unchanged, this method returns immediately without affecting playback.

      Supported state transitions:

      • MENU â†’ plays the menu section
      • GAME_START â†’ plays the game-start section
      • GAME_START_2 â†’ plays the second game-start section
      • GAME_MIDDLE â†’ plays the mid-game section
      • GAME_FINAL â†’ plays the final-game section

    • setVolume

      public void setVolume(double volume)
      Adjusts the playback volume of the underlying media player.
      Parameters:
      volume - the desired volume level, in the range [0.0, 1.0] where 0.0 is silent and 1.0 is full volume
